title: Summer Travel Faces Challenges as Fuel Prices Rise
subtitle: Global conflict and energy costs are changing how people plan their vacations this year.

summary.en: Rising oil prices caused by the war in Iran are making travel more expensive for people around the world. In the United States, the closure of budget airlines like Spirit shows the severe impact of high fuel costs. Meanwhile, in the United Kingdom, many travelers are choosing to stay home for their holidays instead of going abroad. Experts warn that these high energy costs will likely continue to affect travel plans for months. People are now looking for local alternatives to avoid the high costs and uncertainty of international trips.
summary.zh: 伊朗戰爭導致油價上漲，使得全球民眾的旅遊成本變得更加昂貴。在美國，精神航空等廉價航空的倒閉，顯示了高燃料成本帶來的嚴重衝擊。與此同時，在英國，許多旅客選擇留在家鄉度假而非出國。專家警告，這些高昂的能源成本可能會在未來數月持續影響旅遊計畫。人們目前正尋求在地替代方案，以避免國際旅遊的高昂成本與不確定性。

paragraphs.en: Summer is usually a time for excitement and travel, but this year, many people are facing a difficult reality. As energy costs rise, the dream of an affordable vacation is becoming harder to reach. The main reason for this change is the ongoing war in Iran, which has caused oil prices to jump by more than 30%. This increase started when the Strait of Hormuz—a narrow sea passage that is very important for the world’s oil supply—was affected by the conflict. Because so much of the world’s oil passes through this area, the impact has been felt in almost every country.
paragraphs.zh: 夏季通常是令人興奮且適合旅遊的季節，但今年許多人正面臨嚴峻的現實。隨著能源成本攀升，負擔得起的假期變得遙不可及。造成這種變化的主要原因是伊朗持續進行的戰爭，導致油價飆漲超過百分之三十。這波漲勢始於荷莫茲海峽（Strait of Hormuz）受到衝突影響，該海峽是一條對全球石油供應至關重要的狹窄水道。由於全球大部分的石油都經由該地區運輸，幾乎每個國家都感受到了衝擊。

paragraphs.en: In the United States, the situation has become very serious for travelers. According to The Guardian, the national average price for gasoline has reached $4.56 a gallon, which is more than $1 higher than it was last year. In some parts of the country, prices have even gone above $6 a gallon. This has created a difficult environment for families trying to plan their annual trips. For example, Chelsea Blackmore, a photographer from Massachusetts, recently had her travel plans ruined when Spirit Airlines suddenly stopped all operations. The company stated that the high cost of jet fuel was the main reason they could no longer afford to fly.
paragraphs.zh: 在美國，旅遊情況已變得相當嚴峻。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，全國汽油平均價格已達到每加侖四點五六美元，比去年高出超過一美元。在國內部分地區，價格甚至超過每加侖六美元。這對計畫年度旅遊的家庭來說，創造了艱難的環境。例如，來自麻薩諸塞州的攝影師雀兒喜·布萊克摩爾（Chelsea Blackmore），其旅遊計畫近期因精神航空（Spirit Airlines）突然停止所有營運而泡湯。該公司表示，航空燃油成本過高是他們無法繼續營運的主因。

paragraphs.en: Experts believe that the closure of Spirit is just the beginning of a larger problem. Lindsay Owens, the executive director of Groundwork Collaborative, an economic research group, noted that many Americans feel like they are being hit hard by these rising costs. While larger airlines like United and Delta have more money to handle these price increases, smaller budget airlines often operate with very little profit. Without government help, these smaller companies struggle to stay in business. Industry experts warn that because there is now less competition among airlines, passengers will likely have to pay more for their tickets on many routes this summer.
paragraphs.zh: 專家認為，精神航空的倒閉只是更大問題的開端。經濟研究組織「基礎工作合作社」（Groundwork Collaborative）執行董事琳賽·歐文斯（Lindsay Owens）指出，許多美國人感覺自己正深受這些上漲成本的打擊。雖然聯合航空（United Airlines）和達美航空（Delta Air Lines）等大型航空公司有更多資金來應對價格上漲，但小型廉價航空通常在利潤微薄的情況下運作。若無政府協助，這些小型公司難以維持經營。產業專家警告，由於航空公司之間的競爭減少，今年夏天乘客在許多航線上可能必須支付更高的票價。

paragraphs.en: Across the Atlantic, the United Kingdom is seeing a different reaction to these global pressures. Because the war in Iran has made flights and package holidays to popular European destinations more expensive, many British citizens are choosing a “staycation”—a holiday spent in one’s own country. According to BBC News, major booking websites like Booking.com and Airbnb have reported a large increase in searches for domestic trips. Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has even suggested that people might need to change their holiday plans this year due to the changing global situation.
paragraphs.zh: 在大西洋彼岸，英國對於這些全球壓力有著不同的反應。由於伊朗的戰爭使得前往熱門歐洲目的地的航班與套裝行程變得更加昂貴，許多英國公民選擇「宅度假」（staycation），即在自己國家內度過假期。根據英國廣播公司新聞（BBC News）報導，Booking.com 和 Airbnb 等大型訂房網站顯示，國內旅遊的搜尋量大幅增加。英國首相施凱爾（Sir Keir Starmer）甚至暗示，由於全球局勢變動，民眾今年可能需要調整假期計畫。

paragraphs.en: For some, this shift is a chance to discover local beauty. Eboni Dixon, a travel content creator, told BBC News that the UK has many stunning coastlines that can compete with locations abroad. She suggests that people do not always need to get on a plane to have a great holiday experience. Other travelers, like David and Barbara Land, decided to cancel their trip to the Maldives because of government advice against traveling to the United Arab Emirates. Instead, they chose to visit Northumberland in the north of England. While they admit the weather is not as warm as the Maldives, they are looking forward to enjoying local history, walks, and restaurants.
paragraphs.zh: 對某些人來說，這種轉變是發掘在地之美的機會。旅遊內容創作者艾波妮·狄克森（Eboni Dixon）向 BBC 新聞表示，英國擁有許多足以媲美國外景點的絕美海岸線。她建議民眾不一定要搭飛機才能擁有美好的度假體驗。其他旅客如大衛與芭芭拉·蘭德夫婦（David and Barbara Land），因政府建議避免前往阿拉伯聯合大公國，決定取消馬爾地夫之旅。取而代之的是，他們選擇造訪英格蘭北部的諾森伯蘭郡。雖然他們承認天氣不如馬爾地夫溫暖，但他們很期待享受當地的歷史、步道與餐廳。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the outlook remains uncertain. Although some political leaders have spoken about possible peace deals, experts warn that it could take a long time to restore normal energy production in the Gulf region. This means that high fuel prices are likely to stay for the foreseeable future. For travelers, this suggests that the days of cheap, easy international travel may be on hold. Whether it is by paying more for airfare or choosing to explore hidden gems closer to home, people are finding new ways to adapt to a world where energy costs are changing the way we move and relax.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，前景依然不明朗。儘管部分政治領袖已談及可能的和平協議，但專家警告，要恢復波斯灣地區正常的能源生產可能需要很長一段時間。這意味著高燃料價格在可預見的未來可能會持續存在。對旅客而言，這暗示著廉價且輕鬆的國際旅遊時代可能暫時告終。無論是支付更高的機票費用，還是選擇探索家鄉附近隱藏的景點，人們正在尋找新的方式來適應能源成本正在改變我們移動與休閒方式的世界。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, the economic impact of the war is creating a ripple effect that touches everyone. From the driver at the gas pump to the family planning a flight, the cost of energy is now a central part of every decision. As the summer season continues, travelers will need to be flexible and careful with their budgets. While the current situation is difficult, the shift toward local travel shows that people are still determined to find ways to enjoy their time off, even when the global economy makes it harder to do so.
paragraphs.zh: 歸根究柢，戰爭帶來的經濟衝擊正產生漣漪效應，影響著每一個人。從加油站的駕駛到規劃航班的家庭，能源成本現在已成為每項決策的核心部分。隨著夏季持續，旅客需要保持彈性並謹慎規劃預算。雖然目前情況艱難，但轉向在地旅遊顯示，即使全球經濟讓休閒變得更困難，人們仍決心找到享受假期的方式。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To increase suddenly and by a large amount.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞或動詞（跳躍），這裡指價格飆升。文中：The main reason for this change is the ongoing war in Iran, which has caused oil prices to jump by more than 30%.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be severely affected by something negative.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡的 hit 並非物理上的撞擊，而是指受到經濟上的負面衝擊。文中：Lindsay Owens, the executive director of Groundwork Collaborative, an economic research group, noted that many Americans feel like they are being hit hard by these rising costs.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Delayed or paused; not happening at the moment.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於電話用語（保留通話），這裡指計畫被暫時擱置。文中：For travelers, this suggests that the days of cheap, easy international travel may be on hold.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A situation in which one event produces effects which spread and produce further effects.
keyTerms.noteZh: 比喻用法，形容單一事件引發的一連串後續影響。文中：Ultimately, the economic impact of the war is creating a ripple effect that touches everyone.

sources.name: The Guardian Business
sources.title: Spiritless summer: Americans feel squeeze of costly fuel amid busy travel season

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: The UK is set for a staycation summer - and there are plenty of hidden gems

quiz.question: What is the primary reason mentioned for the recent increase in global oil prices?

options.A: A decrease in oil production by major global suppliers.
options.B: A conflict in Iran affecting the Strait of Hormuz.
options.C: Increased demand for air travel during the summer season.
options.D: The sudden closure of several budget airline companies.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第一段，文中明確指出油價上漲的主要原因是伊朗的戰爭，且該衝突影響了全球石油供應的重要通道——荷姆茲海峽。選項 A 文章未提及產量減少；選項 C 是油價上漲的結果而非原因；選項 D 是航空公司倒閉的結果，與油價上漲的根本原因無關。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, what will likely happen to the cost of air travel this summer?

options.A: It will decrease as more people choose to take 'staycations'.
options.B: It will remain stable because large airlines have enough money to absorb costs.
options.C: It will likely increase due to reduced competition among airlines.
options.D: It will become more affordable as airlines offer discounts to attract passengers.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第三段，專家指出小型廉價航空因無法負擔高昂的燃料成本而面臨倒閉，這導致航空公司之間的競爭減少。根據經濟學原理，競爭減少通常會導致價格上漲，因此推論機票價格將會上升。選項 A 錯誤，因為雖然有人選擇國內旅遊，但機票供應減少會推高價格；選項 B 忽略了小型航空倒閉帶來的市場影響；選項 D 與文章提到的市場趨勢相反。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the phrase 'ripple effect' mean in the context of the economic impact of the war?

options.A: A sudden, violent change in the global stock market.
options.B: A series of small, disconnected problems that eventually disappear.
options.C: A situation where one event causes a chain of secondary consequences.
options.D: A peaceful transition to a new way of managing energy resources.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。「Ripple effect」（漣漪效應）在文中用來形容戰爭對經濟產生的連鎖反應，即一個事件（戰爭與油價上漲）導致了一連串後續的經濟影響（如機票變貴、旅遊計畫改變、小型航空倒閉等）。選項 A 過於誇大；選項 B 誤解了漣漪效應的擴散性；選項 D 與文章強調的困難現實不符。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article?

options.A: The war in Iran is the only reason people are choosing to stay home this summer.
options.B: The airline industry is the only sector suffering from the current global energy crisis.
options.C: Rising energy costs are forcing travelers to adapt their plans and change how they experience vacations.
options.D: Government leaders are successfully solving the energy crisis by encouraging domestic tourism.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文的核心主旨是探討高昂的能源成本如何影響全球旅遊業，迫使民眾調整旅遊計畫（如國內旅遊、取消行程等）以適應新的經濟現實。選項 A 過於狹隘，未涵蓋旅遊方式的改變；選項 B 錯誤，因為文中提到這影響了各行各業；選項 D 錯誤，因為政府只是建議，並非解決了能源危機。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
